facts.object_value: Sector-matched counterfactual alpha recompute replacing SPY benchmark per arg 78 T301 sev 7 open Daubert prong (d) asset-classification attack. Universe: Khanna household trades in the 14 canonical pharma/biotech tickers (ABBV/AMGN/BIIB/BMY/GILD/INCY/JNJ/LLY/MRK/MRNA/PFE/REGN/TEVA/VRTX) sourced from lake.house_ptr_transactions_canonical (tx-grain amendment-cascade dedup; n_trades=748; 13/14 ticker price series present — TEVA missing from lake.stock_ohlc_daily). Pricing: pharma ticker adj_close from lake.stock_ohlc_daily; ETF adj_close (XLV/IBB/VHT/SPY) from yfinance live fetch anchor 2026-04-17 (staged for lake.stock_ohlc_daily ingest via queue_for_ingest under source_key yfinance_sector_etfs_xlv_ibb_vht_spy_s04). Per-trade dollar-midpoint notional with sign convention BUY=+1/SELL=-1; entry-price = first trading-day close >= tx_date; anchor-price = last trading-day close <= 2026-04-17; matched sector composite = large-cap pharma → XLV, biotech → IBB. RESULTS (USD P&L): actual Khanna 14-ticker pharma portfolio = +1,385,275; SPY benchmark = +2,636,212 (alpha_vs_SPY = −1,250,937); XLV benchmark = +1,170,181 (alpha_vs_XLV = +215,094); IBB benchmark = +947,806 (alpha_vs_IBB = +437,469); VHT benchmark = +1,112,638 (alpha_vs_VHT = +272,637); matched sector composite benchmark = +1,120,742 (alpha_vs_matched = +264,533). MATERIAL FINDING: under sector-matched benchmark (XLV/IBB/VHT/composite), Khanna 14-ticker pharma-portfolio alpha is POSITIVE (≈ +$215K to +$437K range, +$265K matched composite), whereas under SPY benchmark alpha is NEGATIVE (−$1.25M). The ≈$1.5M swing between SPY-alpha and sector-matched-alpha is pure pharma-sector drift (the pharma sector lagged SPY materially 2017-2026) — NOT trade-selection skill or deficit. This partially CORROBORATES arg 78 prong (d) Daubert attack: Exhibit F §6 '-$2,345,612 sector alpha' framing (which benchmarks against SPY) conflates sector drift with trade-selection, and under a sector-matched benchmark the trade-selection signal on the 14-ticker universe is roughly flat-to-slightly-positive rather than a large loss. NOTE ON SCOPE: Exhibit F §6 uses 1,115 trades across a broader 'pharmaceutical-sector' definition (likely includes XLV/IBB/VHT ETF holdings + additional ticker names beyond the 14-ticker list); this recompute uses only 748 trades in the strict 14-ticker universe, so numbers are not directly comparable in magnitude, only in direction (SPY alpha negative preserved both scopes). Follow-on mining task registered for full 1,115 universe (§8(g) escalation per s02/s03 precedent).

facts.object_value:
FULL-UNIVERSE SECTOR-MATCHED ALPHA RECOMPUTE PER ARG 78 (Option 3 companion to s04 AF #161) — Exhibit F §6 asserts "Pharmaceutical-sector household trading — 1,115 transactions in scope — produced midpoint terminal profit of $8,281,138 against an SPY-benchmark of $10,626,750, yielding sector alpha of minus $2,345,612." Full-universe recompute from lake.house_ptr_transactions_canonical (member_last_name='Khanna'; date range 2017-01-03..2026-04-17; joined to ro_khanna.ticker_map sector='Pharma' 12 tickers ABBV/AMGN/BIIB/GILD/INCY/JNJ/LLY/MRK/MRNA/PFE/REGN/VRTX) per-trade dollar-midpoint × sign (BUY=+1/SELL=-1) × (terminal-return − benchmark-return), benchmarks: SPY / XLV / IBB / VHT / matched composite (XLV for big-pharma ABBV/AMGN/GILD/JNJ/LLY/MRK/PFE; IBB for biotech BIIB/INCY/MRNA/REGN/VRTX):

STRICT PHARMA-12 UNIVERSE (1,088 priced trades; $11.72M notional; matches F212 108-constituent scope):
- actual_pnl_terminal = +$3,175,976
- spy_baseline = +$3,422,735; alpha_vs_SPY = **−$246,759**
- xlv_baseline = +$1,319,675; alpha_vs_XLV = **+$1,856,301**
- ibb_baseline = +$1,380,730; alpha_vs_IBB = **+$1,795,246**
- vht_baseline = +$1,295,498; alpha_vs_VHT = **+$1,880,478**
- matched_composite_baseline = alpha_vs_matched = **+$1,837,077**

BROADER PHARMA+DEVICES+SERVICES UNIVERSE (1,835 priced trades; $19.56M notional; approximates Exhibit F §6 "1,115 trades in scope" more generously via Pharma-12 + Healthcare Devices ABT/BSX/DXCM/ISRG/MDT/SYK + Healthcare Services HCA/HUM):
- actual_pnl_terminal = +$4,262,913
- spy_baseline = +$5,698,292; alpha_vs_SPY = **−$1,435,378**
- alpha_vs_XLV = +$2,157,791
- alpha_vs_IBB = +$1,957,483
- alpha_vs_VHT = +$2,186,204
- alpha_vs_matched = **+$2,138,624**

EXHIBIT F §6 CLAIM (SPY benchmark only): terminal profit $8,281,138 / SPY baseline $10,626,750 / alpha_vs_SPY −$2,345,612.

DIRECTION AND MAGNITUDE: (1) Under SPY benchmark, reproduced SPY-alpha is −$247K (strict Pharma-12) to −$1.44M (broader Healthcare 20-ticker) — smaller in magnitude than the exhibit's −$2.35M by factor of 1.6× to 9.5×. (2) Under any PHARMA-SECTOR benchmark (XLV, IBB, VHT, or the XLV/IBB/VHT matched composite), alpha is strongly POSITIVE across both universes: +$1.80M to +$1.88M on Pharma-12; +$1.96M to +$2.19M on broader healthcare. (3) The matched-composite sign-flip from s04 (14-ticker 748-trade subset, +$264,533) to this s13 broader universe (+$1.84M on 1,088 trades / +$2.14M on 1,835 trades) scales with universe breadth and is robust.

CONSEQUENCE FOR ARG 78 PRONG (d) DAUBERT ASSET-CLASSIFICATION ATTACK: the "$2.35M sector alpha" in Exhibit F §6 body text is SPY-specific. Replacing SPY (a broad-market benchmark) with a sector-appropriate benchmark (XLV/IBB/VHT, which are the standard Daubert-admissible pharma/biotech/healthcare benchmarks for event-study alpha per MacKinlay 1997 JEL Ch. 4 + Campbell-Lo-MacKinlay 1997 Ch. 4.2 — seeded as F824/F825 in s08) reverses the sign from strongly negative to strongly positive. The "trading through events" narrative rescue in §6 body text ("negative alpha is not inconsistent with the regulatory-timing hypothesis... the regulatory-timing signal is independent of directional accuracy") is unnecessary under sector-matched benchmarks — actual alpha is positive, not negative, when measured against pharma's own sector return.

Arg 78 prong (d) Daubert attack EMPIRICALLY STRENGTHENED; full-universe result amplifies s04's partial corroboration (s04 on 14-ticker 748-trade subset showed alpha_vs_matched=+$265K). Full-universe result per Option 3: +$1.84M to +$2.14M on broader universe. Exhibit F §6 requires brief-refresh rewrite per CLAUDE.md §Factual integrity — either to report dual-benchmark (SPY alpha + sector-matched alpha) disclosure or to re-anchor §6 narrative onto sector-matched alpha + narrower re-scoping of the "trading through events" framing. Sharper anchors in Exhibit F remain (§2 NDAA event saturation; §4 repeating-pattern; §5 Oversight-Committee jurisdictional nexus; §7 3-of-3 third-party validation 11-of-12). Per SESSION_PROMPT §8(h) this mining session does not edit outputs/leads/exhibit_packets/**; AF routes brief-refresh cascade.
